title: Boulter and Venus Williams Triumph in Madrid Doubles Debut
summary: British tennis player Katie Boulter teamed up with seven-time Grand Slam champion Venus Williams for the women's doubles at the Madrid Open. The pair, who had never played together before, received a wildcard entry and defeated China's Jiang Xinyu and Xu Yifan in three sets (4-6, 6-3, 10-7). They will next face Taiwan's Hsieh Su-wei and American Sofia Kenin. Boulter, 29, expressed nerves before the match but praised Williams as an "absolute icon." Williams, 45, who is on an 11-match losing streak in singles, said Boulter's energy reminded her of her sister Serena. Boulter is engaged to fellow tennis player Alex de Minaur, and Williams offered marriage advice, telling her to take time and enjoy the moment. In singles action, world number four Iga Swiatek retired due to illness while trailing 3-0 in the third set against Ann Li.

background_read: The Madrid Open is a professional tennis tournament played on clay courts. Venus Williams, at 45, is one of the oldest active players on the WTA tour. She has won seven Grand Slam singles titles and numerous doubles titles with her sister Serena. Katie Boulter is the British number two and has won one WTA singles title. The tournament features both singles and doubles events.
Article_Structure: Main Points: Katie Boulter and Venus Williams won their first-round doubles match at the Madrid Open, defeating a Chinese pair. Boulter expressed nerves and admiration for Williams, who offered marriage advice. In singles, Iga Swiatek retired due to illness. Purpose: To report on the partnership between a rising British player and a tennis legend, highlighting their successful debut and personal interactions. Evidence Evaluation: The article relies on direct quotes from the players and match results, providing credible first-hand accounts. Author Credibility: BBC Sport is a reputable sports news source. Methodology: The article uses match statistics and player interviews to present the story.

perspectives.description: She felt privileged and nervous to play with Venus Williams, whom she considers an icon.
She enjoyed the match and praised Boulter's energy, comparing it to Serena Williams.
